title: TC001-Dashboard loads with header, room list, and empty prompt
description: Verifies the authenticated dashboard shell renders and prompts the user to select a room before any room is chosen.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Navigate to /dashboard (explicit test step) to check the authenticated dashboard shell rendering.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/dashboard")
        
        # -> Fill the sign-in form with provided credentials (admin@test.com / Test@123) and click Sign In to authenticate.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('admin@test.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/div/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Test@123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign In' button to submit the form and wait for the app to render the authenticated dashboard (then verify Header, Sidebar, Room list, and the text prompting to select a room).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Navigate explicitly to /dashboard (per test step). After navigation, check whether the dashboard shell renders and whether the prompt 'Select a room to start chatting' is present; if the dashboard is not accessible or elements are missing, report the issue and finish.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/dashboard")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Header')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Header' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Sidebar')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Sidebar' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Room list')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Room list' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Select a room to start chatting')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Select a room to start chatting' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Navigation to /dashboard redirected to the login page instead of rendering the authenticated dashboard.
- Sign-in submissions did not authenticate: 'Sign In' was submitted (2 attempts) but no redirect to /dashboard occurred.
- Dashboard shell elements (Header, Sidebar, Room list) are not present on the current page.
- The prompt text 'Select a room to start chatting' is not visible on the page.

title: TC002-Select a room and see message history rendered
description: Verifies selecting a room from the sidebar loads and renders the room’s message history in the chat panel.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Navigate to /dashboard. If redirected to login, sign in with provided credentials and then click the first room in the sidebar.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/dashboard")
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('admin@test.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/div/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Test@123')
        
        # -> Fill Email and Password using stored credentials and click the Sign In button to authenticate (fill inputs [68], [69] and click [72]).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('admin@test.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/div/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Test@123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the Sign In button to attempt authentication again, then wait for the page to transition to the dashboard.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Chat panel')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Chat panel' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Message list')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Message list' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Message composer')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Message composer' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Login failed - 'Invalid credentials' notification displayed after submitting stored credentials.
- Dashboard not accessible - navigating to /dashboard redirected to the login page instead of loading the dashboard.
- Cannot select a room - sidebar room list is not available because the user is not authenticated and the dashboard did not load.
- Required UI elements (chat panel, message list, message composer) are not available for verification because login did not complete.

title: TC003-Switch rooms and ensure the selected room changes
description: Verifies switching from one room to another updates the UI to reflect the newly selected room and its message list.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Navigate to /dashboard (explicit test step). After navigation, if a login form is shown, use provided credentials to sign in per authentication handling instructions.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/dashboard")
        
        # -> Fill the Email and Password fields with provided credentials and click Sign In to reach the dashboard.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('admin@test.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/div/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Test@123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the Sign In button (index 68) to attempt login again, then wait for the dashboard to load.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Selected room indicator')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Selected room indicator' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Selected room indicator')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Selected room indicator' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Login failed: 'Invalid credentials' notification displayed after submitting credentials.
- Dashboard not reachable: no dashboard content or sidebar visible after two login attempts.
- Room-switch test could not be executed because the sidebar room list and message list are not accessible without a successful login.

title: TC004-Auto-scroll positions the chat view at the latest messages after room load
description: Verifies that after loading a room, the chat panel shows the most recent messages (auto-scrolled to bottom).
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Sign in using the provided credentials (admin@test.com / Test@123) by filling the Email and Password fields and clicking Sign In.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('admin@test.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/div/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Test@123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the Sign In button to submit credentials and wait for the dashboard to load.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Navigate explicitly to /dashboard as requested by the test steps.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/dashboard")
        
        # -> Navigate explicitly to /dashboard (as the test step requests), then proceed to open the first room.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/dashboard")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Message list')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Message list' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Latest message')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Latest message' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Message composer')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Message composer' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC005-Room list is visible and selectable after initial dashboard load
description: Verifies the sidebar room list is interactive and allows selection without breaking the dashboard layout.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Navigate to /dashboard (http://localhost:5173/dashboard) to check if the sidebar room list exists and is interactive.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/dashboard")
        
        # -> Fill the Email and Password fields with the provided credentials and click the Sign In button to reach the dashboard (use admin@test.com / Test@123).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('admin@test.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/div/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Test@123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Fill Email and Password with admin@test.com / Test@123 and click Sign In to attempt to reach the dashboard.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('admin@test.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/div/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Test@123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Room list')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Room list' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Chat panel')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Chat panel' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Message list')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Message list' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Sign-in form remains visible after submitting credentials; dashboard page did not load.
- "Signing in..." indicator persists and no navigation to /dashboard occurred.
- Sidebar room list cannot be verified because the application remained on the login page.
- Multiple login attempts (2) did not result in successful authentication or navigation to the dashboard.
